summary: We recommend shipping .claude dispatch json as a thin declarative manifest that maps three GitHub PR event shapes to the three review subagents plus a fallback for unknown events because that keeps the dispatch policy auditable and lets the heartbeat or a future webhook receiver consult one canonical file. The tradeoff against embedding the dispatch logic inside each subagent role spec is that having one file makes E4 testable in isolation via a 9-case jq-driven harness without spawning any subagent. The harness scripts dispatch-test sh runs in under a second writes outcomes runrocs E4-dispatch-test log and exits non-zero on any mismatch so a future PR that breaks the manifest hard-fails CI.

claims.statement: .claude dispatch json declares three event entries pull_request opened mapping to outcome-reviewer check_suite completed mapping to ci-firefighter and workflow_run completed mapping to rubric-tightener each with matcher rationale and dedup fields because the manifest source enumerates exactly those three event blocks plus a fallback entry.

citations.title: dispatch json manifest with three event entries and a fallback
agents directory containing the three subagent role specs the manifest references

claims.caveats: The dedup field is documentation only; each subagent enforces its own dedup per its role spec which is intentional so the manifest is not the source of truth for dedup logic.

claims.statement: scripts dispatch-test sh exercises 9 distinct event shapes covering pull_request opened with and without a pair check_suite outcome-gate and typecheck failures plus a success case workflow_run eval-matrix success plus an unrelated workflow case and an unknown event type because the harness file declares exactly 9 assert_eq invocations and the run output lists 9 PASS lines.

citations.title: dispatch-test sh source with 9 assert_eq invocations
E4 dispatch-test log records 9 of 9 passing

claims.caveats: The harness uses a deliberately simple matcher that walks events in declared order and returns the first subagent whose matcher fields are consistent with the event; production webhook handlers may need richer matching but the test set covers the documented event shapes.

claims.statement: running scripts dispatch-test sh on the committed dispatch json produces 9 of 9 passing with exit code 0 because the test harness writes a PASS line for each successful assertion and the run summary line records 9 tests 9 pass 0 fail and the script only exits non-zero if FAIL is greater than 0.

citations.title: E4 runroc captures all 9 PASS lines and the summary
dispatch-test sh exit-on-failure logic at the end of the file

claims.caveats: If a future PR adds a new event type to dispatch json without a corresponding test case the harness will still pass; the orchestrator should treat the harness as a regression guard not as an exhaustiveness check until a follow-up tick adds a coverage assertion.

open_questions: Should the dispatch harness also assert that each subagent name in the manifest corresponds to an actual role spec file under .claude agents so a typo in the manifest fails the test?
methodology_notes: Authored the manifest with three event blocks matching the actual subagent role specs then wrote the test harness using jq to evaluate each matcher against a synthetic event JSON. Ran the harness locally and captured the 9-line PASS log as the runroc. No subagent was spawned during the test because the dispatch matrix is evaluated declaratively.
